page: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv6 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The configuration features 96 vCPUs derived from 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2.0 threads per core. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

This server type runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage, which the instance is capable of supporting. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with the server.

In benchmark testing, the D96ls_v6 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities. It achieved a top-tier rating in the PassMark CPU score of 86191.93 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 21482.0. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reached a top-tier throughput of 509.95 GB/sec, and static web serving throughput peaked at 115.45 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth tests showed strong performance, with read-only large block transfers reaching 257.01 GB/sec. The hardware's AMX and AVX capabilities contribute to strong performance in extended instruction tests and prompt processing for large language models, though text generation speeds remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96ls_v6 offers a high density of compute resources relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory arrays. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring external storage attachment. This server is suited for compute-intensive workloads, including software compilation, web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D96ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C x86_64 processor with 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 4.2 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of memory (2.0 GB per core). The instance lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage. In benchmarks, the D96ls_v6 delivers top-tier multi-core performance, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 86191.93 and a static web serving throughput of 115.45 GB/sec. It also exhibits strong memory bandwidth and efficient execution of extended instruction sets via Intel AMX and AVX-512. This server is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and parallel data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, 192 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for intensive CPU workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v6 is a compute-optimized VM with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, built for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six virtual cores and advanced matrix extensions for demanding parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core performance in PassMark and Geekbench benchmarks
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage attachment
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and parallel processing
